mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL is running hot while FEDFUNDS stays near the zero lower bound, real yields and inflation expectations rise faster than policy restraint, pushing the 10-year Treasury yield up over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises year-over-year while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
failure_condition: Reject if CPIAUCSL acceleration under FEDFUNDS below 0.25% is followed by flat or falling 10-year yields in most triggered cases, or if the sign of the 20-day yield response is negative more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in UNRATE indicates tighter labor markets and stronger nominal growth pressure, which lifts expected policy rates and pulls the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE declines over a monthly window but the 10-year yield falls over the following 20 trading days in a majority of observations, or if rolling rank correlation stays below 0.03 for 3 consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in RSAFS signals stronger consumer demand and firmer nominal activity, which raises expected growth and inflation and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield up within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly growth turns positive and exceeds its prior 6-month median
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS growth is not associated with a positive 20-day yield response, or if the sign agreement falls below 55% after trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in INDPRO and HOUST signals broad real activity and capacity utilization, increasing term-premium and growth expectations; together they should lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined positive-signal state for INDPRO and HOUST does not outperform either series alone, or if 20-day yields do not rise in the majority of joint-strength observations.

mechanism_narrative: If GDPC1 prints above its prior trend while PAYEMS keeps rising, the market infers durable real demand and stronger labor income, which should increase the 10-year Treasury yield over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 year-over-year growth is above its 4-quarter moving average and PAYEMS is rising on a 3-month basis
failure_condition: Reject if above-trend GDP with rising payrolls does not precede higher 60-day Treasury yields, or if the joint condition has a negative average yield response over any full cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A recent increase in FEDFUNDS, especially after a long period of zero rates, signals policy normalization that should push short-rate expectations and the 10-year Treasury yield higher within 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.25 percentage points from its prior month
failure_condition: Reject if rate-hike months are not followed by positive 20-day yield changes more often than not, or if the average response is indistinguishable from zero after the trigger.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with falling PAYEMS identifies labor-market deterioration, which increases recession odds and pushes the 10-year Treasury yield lower over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months while PAYEMS declines over the same window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration state does not precede lower 20-day yields, or if yield changes are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-recovery phase, accelerating INDPRO without a matching rise in HOUST signals supply-side tightening and higher capacity usage, which should raise inflation risk premia and lift the 10-year Treasury yield over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if post-recovery industrial acceleration without housing construction response does not lead to higher 60-day yields, or if the sign of the yield response is negative across a majority of such episodes.
